compiled_state.author: Iso Tester
compiled_state.description:
# WS6 — Rewire the Gerrit review-bot to the four-pass code review

## Why
Epic `d251` (AWS Gerrit + rebar MCP) **shipped and merged** — including S4b's complete review-bot:
`src/rebar/review_bot/` (receiver `app.py`, `voter.py`, `gerrit_client.py`, `dedup.py`,
`reconcile.py`) plus the **verdict→label seam `adapter.code_review_decision(diff_text, repo_root,
ref) → {decision, message, findings}`**. That seam is currently implemented over the OLD
single-pass `rebar.llm.review_code(...)` + a blocking-severity heuristic — a deliberate "proven
pipe" placeholder. `adapter.py`'s own docstring says: *"b744-WS6 will REIMPLEMENT this same
signature over `gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict` — drop-in, with no caller change."*

WS6 is exactly that swap: make Gerrit's `LLM-Review` vote driven by **our new four-pass code
review** (the deterministic Pass-3 blocker), not the single-pass demo.

## Realignment (2026-06-30) — supersedes the earlier standalone-package plan
The pre-merge WS6 plan (a NEW `src/rebar/gerrit/` voter package + REST client + patchset handler)
is **retired as redundant**: d251 already ships all of that in `src/rebar/review_bot/`. WS6 must
NOT build a parallel package — it CONSUMES the shipped receiver and swaps only the decision engine
behind the existing seam. The live dogfood is split out to WS6-b (`0da5`); the GitHub cutover to
WS7.

## What
- **Rewrite `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py`'s `code_review_decision`** over
  `gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict` (the typed `PASS`/`BLOCK`/`INDETERMINATE` verdict):
  - keep the signature + return shape (`{decision, message, findings}`, plus an additive
    `coverage_gap` flag) so `voter.py` is unchanged;
  - map the verdict fail-closed (below); the four-pass gate's own Pass-3 thresholds
    (`criteria_routing.json`) decide PASS vs BLOCK, so the adapter no longer applies the
    `blocking_severities` severity heuristic (it becomes vestigial for this path);
  - review the receiver's cloned working tree by passing `repo_root` (the security detectors scan
    the changed files there) + the fetched `diff_text`.
- **Add an `enabled: bool | None = None` override to `produce_code_review_verdict`** so the adapter
  can pass `enabled=True` (force-enable): the code-review gate is OFF by default
  (`verify.enable_code_review`), but voter activation is itself the authoritative gate, so the
  per-repo flag would otherwise leave every change with the inert disabled verdict. (ADR 0015.)
- **Update `tests/unit/test_review_bot.py`** adapter tests to drive the four-pass verdict (mock
  `produce_code_review_verdict`) instead of `review_code`.
- **ADR 0015** records the seam reimplementation + the force-enable rationale + the fail-closed
  vote mapping + the coverage-gap message distinction.

## Vote mapping (fail-closed; the gate's Pass-3 owns PASS/BLOCK)
`produce_code_review_verdict` emits three verdicts (PASS/BLOCK/INDETERMINATE) plus an inert
disabled verdict. The adapter collapses them to the receiver's PASS/BLOCK (the voter then casts
`llm_review_max_value` / `llm_review_block_value` from `ReceiverConfig`):

| verdict / state | adapter decision | note |
|---|---|---|
| PASS + full coverage | PASS | advisory findings are surfaced in the message, non-blocking |
| BLOCK (blocking findings) | BLOCK | real finding (`coverage_gap=False`) |
| INDETERMINATE (LLM outage) | BLOCK | fail-closed coverage-gap |
| PASS but `coverage.enabled==false` (inert) | BLOCK | fail-closed coverage-gap (`gate-disabled`) — defense-in-depth |
| fail-closed security-scanner abstain | BLOCK | fail-closed coverage-gap (`scanner`) |
| ANY exception / bad shape | BLOCK | fail-closed coverage-gap (`review-error`) |

## Distinct coverage-gap message (infra vs code)
The vote comment (`ReviewInput.message`, posted by the receiver's `post_vote`) begins with a
machine-parseable tag so an INFRA `-1` is unmistakable from a real-finding `-1`:
`[LLM-Review: PASS]`, `[LLM-Review: BLOCK — finding]`, and
`[LLM-Review: BLOCK — coverage-gap (<gate-disabled|llm-unavailable|scanner|review-error>)]`. The
`coverage_gap` sub-reason is derived deterministically from `verdict.coverage`
(`enabled==false` → gate-disabled; `llm_unavailable` → llm-unavailable; a `security_detectors`
note with `reason=='fail-closed-abstain'` → scanner). A real secret MATCH
(`reason=='detector-finding'`) is a finding, NOT a coverage gap.

## Scope
- IN: the `adapter.py` reimplementation, the `produce_code_review_verdict` `enabled` override, the
  updated `test_review_bot.py` adapter tests, ADR 0015.
- OUT: the receiver/voter/gerrit_client/reconciler (d251 shipped them — unchanged); the live
  end-to-end dogfood (WS6-b, gated on the deployed env); the GitHub cutover (WS7).

## Dependencies
- CONSUMES d251's shipped `review_bot` package + the provisioned `LLM-Review` label / value
  mapping (`ReceiverConfig`). depends_on WS4 (98ff, closed) for `produce_code_review_verdict`.
- No live env needed: the adapter is fully verifiable offline (mock the verdict; the receiver's
  own tests already stub Gerrit).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `review_bot/adapter.py`'s `code_review_decision` is reimplemented over `produce_code_review_verdict` with `enabled=True`; the signature + `{decision, message, findings}` return shape are unchanged (the voter is not touched).
- [ ] Vote mapping is exhaustive + fail-closed over PASS / BLOCK / INDETERMINATE / inert-disabled / scanner-abstain / exception; a test asserts each → the correct decision, and that an inert-disabled verdict never yields PASS.
- [ ] `produce_code_review_verdict` gains an `enabled: bool | None = None` override (default None = read config); a test asserts `enabled=True` forces the gate to run even when `verify.enable_code_review=false`.
- [ ] The vote message distinguishes a coverage-gap `-1` (sub-reason gate-disabled / llm-unavailable / scanner / review-error) from a real-finding `-1`, via the documented first-line tag; a test asserts the PASS tag, the finding tag, and each coverage-gap sub-reason.
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_review_bot.py` adapter tests are updated to the four-pass verdict (mock `produce_code_review_verdict`); the full `pytest -k review_bot` suite passes; ruff + mypy clean.
- [ ] ADR 0015 records the seam reimplementation, the force-enable rationale, the fail-closed vote mapping, and the coverage-gap message vocabulary.

file_impact.reason: Gerrit LLM-Review voter (event wiring + REST vote)

compiled_state.title: WS6: Rewire the Gerrit review-bot to the four-pass code review

verify_commands.command: echo 'manual: push change -> LLM-Review vote -> submit -> replicate'
